content:
Key Findings

  • Wyre Council's spending increased by £74.6M (107.9%) from 2017 to 2025
  • The largest year-over-year increase was £85.6M (479.3%) from 2019 to 2020
  • The largest year-over-year decrease was £60.36M (87.3%) from 2017 to 2018
  • Wyre Council published 51,092 transactions across 8 financial years
  • Spending peaked at £171.6M in 2023 before dropping to £72.1M in 2024

Spending Volatility

Wyre Council's spending has seen significant fluctuations over the past eight years. The most notable increase occurred between 2019 and 2020, where spending surged by £85.6M (479.3%). This sharp rise was followed by a substantial drop of £58.05M (56.1%) the following year. Such volatility raises questions about the consistency of service provision and financial planning.

The largest decrease in spending was observed between 2017 and 2018, with a reduction of £60.36M (87.3%). This dramatic drop was followed by a more moderate increase of £9.1M (104.1%) in the subsequent year. Understanding the reasons behind these fluctuations is crucial for assessing the council's financial stability and transparency.

Yearly Spending Trends

To better understand the spending patterns, let's examine the year-over-year changes in more detail. The table below provides a clear view of the fluctuations in spending over the years.

Year Spending (£) Change (£) Change (%)
2017 £69.1M - -
2018 £8.7M £-60,361,039 -87.3%
2019 £17.9M +£9,100,000 +104.1%
2020 £103.4M +£85,600,000 +479.3%
2021 £45.4M £-58,054,763 -56.1%
2022 £51.1M +£5,700,000 +12.6%
2023 £171.6M +£120,500,000 +235.8%
2024 £72.1M £-99,458,302 -58.0%
2025 £143.7M +£71,500,000 +99.1%

The data reveals a pattern of significant increases followed by sharp decreases, indicating potential areas for further investigation. For instance, the substantial increase in 2020 and subsequent drop in 2021 could be linked to specific projects or external factors that warrant closer examination.

Transparency and Accountability

Wyre Council has published 51,092 transactions over the past eight years, covering a total spend of £683.0M. While this level of detail is commendable, the volatility in spending patterns suggests that there may be room for improved transparency and accountability. Understanding the reasons behind these fluctuations can help ensure that public funds are being used effectively and efficiently.

To enhance transparency, the council could provide more detailed explanations for significant changes in spending. This could include publishing reports that outline the reasons behind major increases or decreases, as well as the impact of these changes on service provision. Additionally, engaging with the public through open forums and consultations can help build trust and ensure that the council's financial decisions are aligned with community needs.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Wyre Council about the reasons behind the significant spending fluctuations observed in 2020 and 2021
  • Attend council committee meetings, which are open to the public, to gain insights into the council's financial decisions and spending priorities
  • Check the council's published contracts register to understand how public funds are being allocated and to whom
  • Write to your local councillor about the need for improved transparency and accountability in Wyre Council's financial decisions
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and gain a deeper understanding of Wyre Council's financial activities
This article is based on Wyre Council's published spending data covering 2017/18 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
